title: CLI command-grammar standard
description:
A written command-grammar standard for the CLI that every command follows, so the conformance fixes have one canonical form to cite.

## Background

The CLI grammar is inconsistent and undocumented today. The gate command surface mixes gate configuration, attachment, execution, and inspection in one flat enum (cli.rs:866-1082), and the `-t` short flag means different things on different commands: `-t`=`--title` on `issue create` (cli.rs:437) versus `-t`=`--doc-type` on `doc add` (cli.rs:1179). Without a single named reference there is no canonical form for the rest of the conformance work to conform to.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-06: A reference document states the command grammar covering positional-versus-flag conventions, id-acceptance semantics across sibling commands, noun/verb structure, and the separation of gate configuration from gate execution and inspection, and it is the named standard the conformance work cites.

## Notes

Docs only, self-contained. The standard must cover positional-vs-flag rules, id-acceptance semantics across sibling commands, noun/verb structure, the gate config-vs-execution/inspection separation, and the `-t` short-flag rule (standardized with `--type` long-only on `issue create`).
